context.problem: When a held position reaches NEAR_RESOLUTION_PRICE (0.97), check_exit() currently closes it immediately for 97¢. With the resolution watcher now live, the bot can hold for full 1.00 payout — Polymarket settles the market and market_resolved fires in the next loop iteration. EXIT_GAME_AWARE_001 attempted this but was BLOCKED because integration/resolution_watcher.py did not exist and caused 3 stuck trades. Both resolution watcher tasks are now APPROVED and the infrastructure is live.
what_is_already_wired: integration/resolution_watcher.py polls Gamma API every 60s and writes runtime/resolved_markets.json
_load_resolved_markets() in bot_core.py: mtime-cached, called at top of exit loop each iteration
market_resolved force-close block runs BEFORE check_exit() — closes at yes_resolution_price or no_resolution_price without needing OB liquidity
resolved_markets dict keyed by market_id: {resolved: bool, yes_resolution_price: float, no_resolution_price: float, ...}
context.what_002_adds: A suppression gate: after check_exit() returns (True, 'near_resolution'), check whether the market is already in resolved_markets. If NOT resolved yet, suppress the exit and hold. The market_resolved block will fire on the next loop once the watcher confirms resolution.
context.safe_degradation: If resolved_markets is empty (watcher not running, file missing, or no open trades polled yet), the suppression must NOT block — fall through to the existing near_resolution close. This preserves the pre-watcher behavior as a backstop.
context.time_exit_backstop: _time_to_end() and TIME_EXIT_BUFFER_SECONDS already exist. If the game ends and Polymarket never resolves the market, the time_exit reason will eventually fire as a backstop close. Do not add a separate timeout — time_exit already covers this.

implementation.where: In the exit loop, immediately after 'should_close, reason = check_exit(trade, ob, t2e)' returns and before 'if should_close:' executes. The resolved_markets variable is already loaded at the top of the exit loop by _load_resolved_markets().
replace_with: # If near_resolution fired but watcher has not confirmed
# settlement yet, suppress the exit and hold for full payout.
# Safe degradation: if resolved_markets is empty (watcher
# not running), fall through to normal near_resolution close.
'HOLD trade=%d %s — near resolution, awaiting Polymarket confirmation',
notes: resolved_markets is already a local variable in the exit loop from the RESOLUTION_WATCHER_INTEGRATE_001 work — do not re-call _load_resolved_markets(), just use the existing variable.
The 'and resolved_markets' guard (truthy dict check) is the safe degradation: empty dict → gate is skipped → near_resolution closes as before.
The 'continue' skips the rest of the loop body for this trade only — moves to the next open trade.
Do NOT suppress stop_loss, gap_stop, take_profit, trailing_stop, time_exit, or market_resolved — only 'near_resolution' is suppressed.

The near_resolution cooldown block (600s) runs inside 'if should_close:' — because we 'continue' before reaching it, no cooldown is set when holding. This is correct: we want the bot free to re-evaluate next loop.
Confirm that resolved_markets is set before the for-trade loop (not inside it) — the INTEGRATE_001 result summary says it is called once per loop iteration at the top.

steps: git diff --name-only  # must show only bot_core.py
Grep check: grep -n 'HOLD trade=' bot_core.py  # confirm log line is present
Grep check: grep -n 'near_resolution' bot_core.py  # confirm suppression gate and cooldown block both present
Logic trace: walk through the three cases manually and confirm correct behavior for each (see below)

case_1_watcher_running_market_not_resolv.state: resolved_markets = {'other_market': ...}, trade.market_id not in resolved_markets, check_exit returns (True, 'near_resolution')
case_1_watcher_running_market_not_resolv.expected: Gate fires, logger.info HOLD, continue — trade stays open

case_2_watcher_running_market_resolved.expected: market_resolved force-close fires BEFORE check_exit is even called — gate never reached for this trade

case_3_watcher_not_running.expected: 'and resolved_markets' is falsy — gate skipped — near_resolution close proceeds as before

case_4_stop_loss_not_suppressed.expected: Gate condition 'reason == near_resolution' is False — gate skipped — stop_loss closes normally

do_not: Touch core/risk.py, integration/resolution_watcher.py, or any file other than bot_core.py
Suppress any exit reason other than near_resolution
Add a separate timeout mechanism — time_exit already covers stuck positions
Set or modify any cooldown for the hold path
Re-call _load_resolved_markets() inside the per-trade loop — use the variable already loaded at loop top
Change NEAR_RESOLUTION_PRICE threshold or any other env var value
